wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on the Dissensus AI debate platform and meme coin ecosystem. Explain the project's purpose as a multi-agent dialectical debate engine combined with blockchain token integration. Document the core philosophy of finding truth through adversarial AI reasoning, the target audience including AI enthusiasts and crypto investors, and the unique value proposition of real-time AI debates. Include the project's vision, key milestones, competitive landscape analysis, and positioning in the AI + blockchain space. Provide context about the 3 AI agents (CIPHER, NOVA, PRISM) and their role in the dialectical process. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction & Vision
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the introduction and vision section focused on the Dissensus AI debate platform. Explain the project's core mission of using multi-agent AI systems to find truth through dialectical reasoning. Document the unique value proposition of combining adversarial AI debates with blockchain token integration. Describe the target audience including AI researchers, crypto enthusiasts, and general users interested in AI-generated content. Explain the philosophical foundation of dialectical methodology and how it applies to AI systems. Include the project's long-term vision, goals, and expected impact on AI education and blockchain adoption. Use accessible language for non-technical stakeholders while maintaining technical accuracy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Microservices Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dissensus microservices architecture. Describe how the system is organized into distinct service layers including the Express.js web server, AI debate engine service, staking service, metrics service, and research engine service. Document the service boundaries, inter-service communication patterns, and dependency relationships. Explain how each microservice handles specific responsibilities: debate orchestration, user access control, analytics tracking, and research processing. Include architectural diagrams showing service interactions, data flow between services, and API gateway patterns. Address service discovery, load balancing strategies, and fault tolerance mechanisms. Document the separation of concerns between frontend presentation layer, backend business logic layer, and external integration layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tier Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the tier management system. Explain the five-tier structure (FREE, BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, WHALE) with their respective minimum stake requirements, daily debate limits, and feature access. Document the tier calculation algorithm, feature permissions matrix, and how users automatically upgrade/downgrade based on staked amounts. Include examples of tier configurations, feature differences between tiers, and the unlimited debate access for Gold and Whale tiers. Provide code examples showing tier determination logic, feature flag implementation, and tier benefit enumeration. Address tier thresholds, stake calculations, and the relationship between token amounts and access privileges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debate Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the debate interface component. Document the real-time debate visualization system showing three AI agents (CIPHER, NOVA, PRISM) in adversarial dialogue. Explain the phase-by-phase debate progression with visual indicators, agent-specific styling and avatars, real-time content streaming, and status indicators. Cover the topic input system with suggestions, API key configuration, provider selection (DeepSeek, Gemini, OpenAI), and model selection. Detail the debate arena layout, agent columns with individual content areas, real-time status updates, and the verdict panel with copy and share functionality. Include examples of customizing agent appearances, modifying phase indicators, and extending the debate visualization with new agents or phases.

wiki_catalogs.name: 4-Phase Dialectical Methodology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the 4-phase dialectical methodology that forms the core of the AI debate system. Explain the theoretical foundation of Hegelian dialectics and how it's adapted for AI debate. Detail each phase: Phase 1 (Independent Analysis) where agents analyze topics privately, Phase 2 (Opening Arguments) where formal positions are presented, Phase 3 (Cross-Examination) where arguments are challenged and refined, and Phase 4 (Final Verdict) where a definitive consensus is reached. Document the rationale behind the sequential structure, the role of each phase in achieving intellectual synthesis, and how the methodology ensures balanced debate outcomes. Include examples of how different topics would progress through each phase and the expected outputs at each stage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Solana Token System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Solana token system integration. Explain the SPL token implementation using the @solana/spl-token library, including mint address configuration, associated token account (ATA) creation, and balance verification processes. Document the fetchDissBalance function workflow, RPC connection handling, and error management strategies. Cover wallet address normalization, public key validation, and token account lookup procedures. Include examples of balance retrieval, decimal handling, and zero-balance scenarios. Address security considerations for blockchain interactions, RPC endpoint configuration, and environment variable management. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common issues like invalid wallets, missing accounts, and network connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web Research Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the web research engine component. Explain the DuckDuckGo web scraping implementation, search result extraction algorithms, and multi-domain research strategies. Document the research_topic function that orchestrates different search approaches based on topic domains (crypto, AI, finance, energy). Detail the web_search function's HTML parsing logic, SSL context handling, and result processing. Include examples of research query construction, domain-specific search strategies, and evidence synthesis. Address the research summary generation process, fact filtering mechanisms, and research text formatting. Provide guidance on extending search capabilities, customizing research parameters, and integrating additional search sources.

wiki_catalogs.name: VPS Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive VPS deployment documentation covering the complete server setup process. Document the PowerShell deployment script functionality, SSH key configuration, PM2 process management, and systemd service setup. Include detailed nginx reverse proxy configuration, SSL certificate installation, firewall setup, and security hardening procedures. Provide step-by-step instructions for environment variable deployment, file permissions, and service restart procedures. Address common deployment issues, troubleshooting steps, and rollback procedures. Document the relationship between deployment scripts and system services, including automatic startup configuration and monitoring setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debate API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the debate system endpoints. Document the debate validation endpoint (/api/debate/validate) for preflight checks including topic validation, provider/model validation, and wallet requirements. Cover the main debate streaming endpoint (/api/debate/stream) with detailed SSE event types, real-time streaming implementation, and parameter specifications. Include request/response schemas for debate initiation, streaming event formats, and error handling. Document rate limiting policies, input validation rules, and integration patterns. Provide practical examples showing client implementation with EventSource, parameter validation workflows, and error recovery strategies. Address performance considerations, connection management, and debugging techniques for real-time debate streaming.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for customizing AI debate agents in the Dissensus platform. Document the AGENTS object structure including personality definitions, system prompts, visual styling options, and behavioral patterns. Explain how to modify existing agent personalities (CIPHER, NOVA, PRISM) by editing their core identity, reasoning style, debate behavior, and signature phrases. Provide step-by-step instructions for creating entirely new agents with custom names, roles, colors, portraits, and system prompts. Include examples of personality modifications for different use cases such as educational settings, corporate analysis, or specialized domains. Document the agent selection mechanism, system prompt injection, and how custom agents integrate with the 4-phase debate methodology. Address best practices for maintaining debate balance and ensuring coherent reasoning patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debate Validation Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the debate validation endpoint (/api/debate/validate). Document the POST request structure with parameters including topic, apiKey, provider, model, and wallet. Explain the preflight validation workflow that checks topic length (3-500 characters), provider/model existence, API key availability, and wallet requirements when staking enforcement is enabled. Detail the response schemas for successful validation ({ok: true}) and various error scenarios including missing parameters, invalid inputs, and staking restrictions. Include practical examples showing client-side validation patterns, error handling strategies, and integration with the main debate streaming endpoint. Address rate limiting considerations and security implications of preflight validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Dissensus platform. Include step-by-step installation instructions for local development, environment setup requirements, and initial configuration. Document the repository structure, prerequisite technologies (Node.js, Python, Solana), and dependency installation process. Provide quick start examples showing how to run the debate engine locally, connect to AI providers, and verify the setup works correctly. Include basic usage examples for initiating debates, understanding the interface, and exploring available features. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for first-time users. Make the content beginner-friendly while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Concepts & Philosophy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core concepts and philosophy section explaining the fundamental principles underlying the Dissensus platform. Document the 4-phase dialectical methodology (thesis, antithesis, synthesis, verdict) and how it's adapted for AI systems. Explain the role of the three AI agents (CIPHER, NOVA, PRISM) and their distinct personalities in creating meaningful debates. Describe the concept of adversarial reasoning and how it leads to more robust conclusions than traditional AI approaches. Include the philosophical basis for using AI to explore truth, the limitations of current AI systems, and how the debate format addresses these challenges. Provide concrete examples from the codebase showing how these concepts translate into practical implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dissensus data flow architecture. Document the complete data journey from user input through AI processing to final output delivery. Explain the flow of debate topics through the validation layer, AI provider integration, real-time streaming via Server-Sent Events, and final verdict delivery. Detail the data transformation pipeline including topic sanitization, provider model selection, streaming response formatting, and client-side rendering. Document the metrics data flow from debate execution through analytics collection to public dashboard display. Include blockchain data flow for wallet verification and staking status checks. Provide data flow diagrams showing synchronous and asynchronous processing paths, error handling flows, and data persistence strategies. Address caching mechanisms, data validation at each stage, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Daily Debate Limits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the daily debate limitation system. Explain the per-tier debate limits (1, 5, 20 debates per day for Bronze/Silver, unlimited for Gold/Whale), the daily reset mechanism, and usage tracking. Document the debate counter logic, date-based reset functionality, and how debatesUsedToday and lastDebateDay properties work together. Include examples of daily limit enforcement, remaining debate calculation, and the special handling of unlimited access tiers. Provide code examples showing canDebate() function implementation, recordDebateUsage() logic, and ensureDailyReset() functionality. Address edge cases like first-time users, limit exceeded scenarios, and reset timing considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the wallet connection and staking integration system. Document the Solana wallet connection process using Phantom and Solflare, public key verification, simulated staking system for demonstration purposes, and tier-based access control. Explain the staking panel functionality including wallet input, stake simulation, tier threshold display, and status management. Cover the integration with the backend staking API, wallet status checking, and enforcement mechanisms. Include examples of connecting different wallet providers, implementing custom wallet adapters, handling connection errors, and extending the staking system for production on-chain integration. Address security considerations, user experience during wallet operations, and fallback mechanisms for offline scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Personalities and Characteristics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the three AI agent personalities (CIPHER, NOVA, PRISM) and their distinct characteristics. Explain the philosophical foundations and behavioral patterns of each agent: CIPHER as the analytical bear (risk-focused, contrarian), NOVA as the optimistic bull (growth-focused, bullish), and PRISM as the impartial synthesizer (balanced, analytical). Document the system prompts, personality traits, argumentation styles, and decision-making patterns for each agent. Include examples of how each agent would approach the same debate topic differently, showing their unique perspectives and reasoning processes. Address the relationship between agent personalities and the overall debate methodology.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tokenomics & Economic Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the $DISS token economic model and tokenomics. Explain the supply distribution, inflation/deflation mechanisms, and utility functions within the platform ecosystem. Document the relationship between token holdings and platform access tiers, including FREE, BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, and WHALE tiers. Cover staking mechanics, daily debate limits, and premium feature access based on token balance. Include the launch strategy, initial distribution, and community incentives. Address the economic sustainability model, revenue streams, and token buyback mechanisms. Provide examples of how tokenomics drive user engagement and platform growth. Document the connection between blockchain activities and platform monetization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Topic Analysis System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the topic analysis and classification system. Explain the analyze_topic function that performs natural language processing on user input to determine question types, domain classification, and research focus areas. Document the regex-based pattern matching for detecting question forms (comparison, exclusivity, prediction, normative statements), domain detection algorithms for crypto, AI, finance, and energy sectors, and sentiment analysis heuristics. Include examples of topic classification outputs, domain mapping logic, and research strategy determination. Address the relationship between topic analysis and research engine routing, customizing analysis rules, and extending domain detection capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed development environment setup documentation covering local development prerequisites, dependency installation, and configuration management. Document Node.js version requirements, npm package installation, environment variable configuration, and local server startup procedures. Include guidance on API key setup for AI providers, database configuration if applicable, and development server hot-reload functionality. Provide troubleshooting steps for common development issues, IDE configuration recommendations, and debugging techniques. Address development workflow best practices, code formatting standards, and contribution guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Staking API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the staking and access control system. Document the tier information endpoint (/api/staking/tiers) for retrieving staking requirements and access levels. Cover the staking status endpoint (/api/staking/status) for wallet verification and tier determination. Include stake/unstake endpoints (/api/staking/stake, /api/staking/unstake) with parameter validation and simulation logic. Document wallet normalization, daily debate limits, and staking enforcement mechanisms. Provide request/response schemas, rate limiting information, and integration examples. Address staking simulation mode, wallet validation requirements, and tier-based access control patterns. Include troubleshooting guidance for common staking issues and client implementation best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme & Branding
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for customizing the visual theme and branding of the Dissensus platform. Document the CSS architecture including color schemes, typography, layout modifications, and responsive design adjustments. Explain how to customize agent-specific styling, character artwork integration, and brand identity elements. Provide guidance on modifying the main stylesheet, adding custom CSS variables, and implementing theme switching functionality. Include examples of logo integration, favicon customization, and social media sharing visuals. Document the relationship between frontend styling and agent visual identities, including how colors and themes reflect agent personalities. Address mobile responsiveness, accessibility considerations, and browser compatibility for custom themes. Provide templates and examples for common branding scenarios such as educational institutions, corporate environments, or community platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debate Streaming Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the debate streaming endpoint (/api/debate/stream). Document the GET request parameters including topic, apiKey, provider, model, and wallet. Explain the Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementation with detailed event types: initial connection establishment, agent responses, evidence sharing, and final verdict announcement. Detail the streaming event format with type field and associated data payloads. Include client implementation examples using EventSource API, connection management, error handling for network interruptions, and proper cleanup procedures. Document rate limiting policies (10 debates per minute in production), input validation rules, and integration patterns with the validation endpoint. Address performance considerations, connection timeouts, and debugging techniques for real-time debate streaming.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation covering all programming languages, frameworks, and external dependencies used in the Dissensus platform. Document the Node.js/Express backend architecture, Python Flask research engine, HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend components, and blockchain integration with Solana. Detail the AI provider integrations (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Google Gemini), external libraries (@solana/web3.js, @resvg/resvg-js, satori), and deployment infrastructure (nginx, systemd). Include version requirements, licensing information, and rationale for technology choices. Provide dependency management guidance, security considerations for external packages, and upgrade paths for critical dependencies. Address both development and production environment requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Competitive Landscape & Positioning
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the competitive landscape and positioning section analyzing the Dissensus platform's market position. Document existing AI debate platforms, content generation tools, and blockchain-based projects. Analyze the unique advantages of the Dissensus approach including real-time streaming, multi-agent coordination, and blockchain integration. Compare with competitors in terms of technology stack, user experience, and innovation. Identify market gaps the platform fills and potential future competition. Include SWOT analysis (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) specific to the AI + blockchain intersection. Document the platform's differentiation factors including the dialectical methodology, token-based access control, and social media integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dissensus integration patterns and external service connectivity. Document how the system integrates with external AI providers (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Google Gemini) using API key management and provider abstraction layers. Explain blockchain integration patterns for Solana wallet verification and token balance checking. Detail the research engine integration with web search APIs and topic analysis services. Document the card generation service integration with image processing libraries and social media sharing workflows. Include integration patterns for rate limiting, error handling, retry mechanisms, and fallback strategies. Address API key security, credential management, and environment-based configuration. Document the plugin architecture for adding new AI providers and external services. Provide integration diagrams showing external service dependencies, communication protocols, and data exchange formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the wallet integration system. Explain the Solana wallet connection process, address normalization, and balance verification workflow. Document the wallet address validation, base58 encoding requirements, and integration with the staking system. Include examples of wallet connection flows, address sanitization, and the transition from simulated staking to production blockchain integration. Provide code examples showing wallet validation logic, address normalization functions, and the relationship between wallet addresses and staking data. Address security considerations, wallet privacy, and the difference between development simulation and production blockchain verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Card Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the social media card generation system. Document the card creation workflow for generating shareable debate content, SVG to PNG conversion process, dynamic content generation based on debate results, and image processing pipeline. Explain the card template system, agent-specific styling for cards, verdict formatting, and social media optimization. Cover the integration with the backend card generator service, real-time card preview, download functionality, and sharing capabilities. Include examples of customizing card templates, adding new visual elements, modifying content layouts, and extending the system for different social media platforms. Address performance optimization for card generation, caching strategies, and mobile-responsive card designs.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI provider integration system that enables pluggable support for multiple LLM providers. Document the PROVIDERS configuration object and how it supports OpenAI, DeepSeek, and Google Gemini APIs. Explain the provider abstraction layer, authentication mechanisms, model configuration, and cost calculation features. Detail the API integration patterns, request formatting, streaming response handling, and error management. Include examples of adding new AI providers, configuring authentication headers, and managing provider-specific parameters. Address provider selection logic, fallback mechanisms, and performance considerations for multi-provider deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Meme Coin Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the meme coin launch infrastructure and marketing automation tools. Document the launch kit components including pre-written social media content, domain recommendations, and pump.fun listing procedures. Explain the Twitter/X setup process, automated tweet scheduling, and community engagement strategies. Cover the relationship between meme coin activities and platform adoption, including cross-promotion tactics and viral marketing campaigns. Include examples of launch sequences, community building workflows, and engagement tracking. Address the technical setup requirements for social media automation, content generation, and campaign management. Document best practices for meme coin launches, regulatory considerations, and risk management in volatile markets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Response Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the multi-agent response generation system that creates AI agent opening statements, cross-examinations, rebuttals, and consensus conclusions. Explain the generate_cipher_opening, generate_nova_opening, and generate_prism_opening functions that create agent-specific responses based on research findings and topic analysis. Document the generate_cross_examination function that produces adversarial questioning between agents, and the generate_rebuttals and generate_consensus functions that handle response refinement and synthesis. Include examples of agent personality expressions, evidence-based argument construction, and debate dynamics. Address the HTML escaping mechanisms, agent-specific writing styles, and response formatting patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and maintenance documentation covering system health monitoring, performance tracking, and operational procedures. Document the metrics dashboard functionality, real-time analytics collection, and usage statistics tracking. Include guidance on log file management, performance optimization techniques, and capacity planning. Provide maintenance schedules, backup procedures, and disaster recovery protocols. Address system alerting, error tracking, and incident response procedures. Document routine maintenance tasks, security updates, and system health checks. Include troubleshooting guides for common operational issues and performance bottlenecks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Solana Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Solana blockchain integration endpoints. Document the token balance endpoint (/api/solana/token-balance) for on-chain $DISS balance verification including wallet parameter validation, error handling, and rate limiting. Cover the staking status endpoint (/api/solana/staking-status) for future stake program integration. Include configuration parameters, cluster settings, and mint address information. Document the server-side architecture that prevents API key exposure to clients. Provide request/response schemas, wallet format requirements, and integration examples. Address security considerations, rate limiting policies, and troubleshooting common blockchain integration issues. Include guidance for local development setup and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for integrating additional AI providers and extending the debate engine's language model capabilities. Document the PROVIDERS object structure, API key configuration, and model selection logic. Explain how to add new AI providers by extending the provider configuration, implementing API wrappers, and handling response formatting. Include step-by-step instructions for integrating providers like OpenAI, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, and other LLM services. Document rate limiting, error handling, fallback mechanisms, and cost optimization strategies. Provide examples of custom provider implementations, authentication methods, and parameter tuning. Address model capability mapping, response validation, and maintaining consistent output formatting across different providers. Include troubleshooting guides for common integration issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dissensus system architecture. Describe the high-level design showing the relationship between the AI debate engine, research platform, frontend applications, and blockchain integration. Document the microservices architecture pattern, component interactions, data flow between modules, and integration points. Explain the Express.js server layer, real-time streaming architecture using Server-Sent Events, Solana blockchain integration layer, and Python-based research engine. Include system context diagrams showing how users interact with the platform, how AI providers are integrated, and how blockchain verification works. Address scalability considerations, load balancing strategies, and deployment topology. Document cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Roadmap & Key Milestones
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the roadmap and key milestones section documenting the project's evolution and future plans. Outline major releases, feature additions, and platform improvements. Document completed milestones including initial platform launch, AI provider integration, blockchain implementation, and community growth. Explain upcoming features such as enhanced AI models, expanded token utilities, research platform integration, and mobile deployment. Include timeline expectations, resource allocation, and dependency relationships between milestones. Address potential risks and mitigation strategies for achieving roadmap objectives. Provide clear indicators of success for each milestone and how they contribute to the overall platform vision.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Dissensus security architecture and threat mitigation strategies. Document the multi-layered security approach including input validation, rate limiting, API key management, and access control mechanisms. Explain the staking-based access control system for preventing abuse and managing user quotas. Detail the security measures for blockchain integration including wallet address validation, transaction safety, and private key protection. Document the Express.js security middleware configuration including helmet headers, CORS policies, and content security policies. Address API security patterns including request validation, parameter sanitization, and error handling without information leakage. Include security considerations for AI provider integration, data privacy, and user authentication. Document security monitoring, logging strategies, and incident response procedures. Provide security architecture diagrams showing trust boundaries, data protection zones, and threat mitigation layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Access Control Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the access control system. Explain how staking tiers translate to feature access, the debate permission system, and API endpoint protection. Document the canDebate() function, access validation workflows, and integration with debate initiation. Include examples of access control checks, permission enforcement, and the relationship between staking status and system features. Provide code examples showing permission validation logic, debate initiation restrictions, and feature gating mechanisms. Address the enforcement mode (STAKING_ENFORCE=1), simulation vs. production behavior, and how access control affects user experience across different tiers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme & Styling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the theme and styling customization system. Document the CSS architecture using custom properties for theming, agent-specific color schemes (CIPHER blue, NOVA orange, PRISM purple), responsive design implementation, and component styling patterns. Explain the modular CSS structure, media queries for mobile responsiveness, animation systems for loading states and transitions, and accessibility features. Cover the customization options including color scheme modification, typography changes, layout adjustments, and brand element replacement. Include examples of creating custom themes, implementing dark/light mode switching, adding new visual effects, and maintaining consistency across components. Address browser compatibility, performance considerations for animations, and responsive breakpoints used throughout the interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-Time Streaming Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the real-time streaming architecture that powers the debate interface. Explain the Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementation, event emission patterns, and client-server communication protocols. Document the streaming callbacks, event types (debate-start, phase-start, agent-chunk, agent-done, debate-done), and data flow patterns. Detail the asynchronous execution model, parallel agent processing, and real-time data aggregation. Include examples of client-side event handling, streaming UI updates, and performance optimization techniques. Address error handling in streaming contexts, connection management, and debugging streaming issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Discussion API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the research discussion endpoints. Document the POST /api/discuss endpoint that processes research queries, performs topic analysis, executes web searches, and generates multi-agent responses. Include request/response schemas, parameter validation, error handling, and rate limiting considerations. Detail the GET /api/health endpoint for service monitoring. Provide examples of API usage patterns, response formats, and integration guidelines. Address CORS configuration, static file serving, and deployment considerations. Include troubleshooting guidance for common API issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Metrics & Analytics API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the metrics and analytics system. Document the integration with the metrics dashboard and real-time analytics capabilities. Provide examples of client integration patterns, data visualization approaches, and performance optimization techniques. Address rate limiting, caching strategies, and data privacy considerations. Include guidance for building custom analytics interfaces and monitoring system health.

Address image quality settings, compression options, and performance considerations. Include troubleshooting guidance for image generation failures and client-side integration best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend documentation covering the user interface and interactive components. Document the debate interface design, real-time visualization of AI conversations, wallet connection integration, and responsive design implementation. Explain the agent-specific styling system, theme customization options, and user interaction patterns. Include the card generator functionality for social media sharing, metrics dashboard presentation, and mobile-responsive layout. Provide examples of customizing themes, adding new visual elements, and extending the interface. Address browser compatibility, performance optimization, and accessibility considerations. Document the integration with backend APIs and real-time streaming events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Research & Discussion Platform
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the research-powered discussion platform. Explain the web research engine that analyzes topics, performs web searches, and synthesizes evidence-based responses. Document the Flask-based backend architecture, topic classification algorithms, and community discussion features. Include examples of research query processing, evidence extraction, and forum moderation. Address the integration between the research engine and the main debate platform, user contribution workflows, and quality control mechanisms. Provide guidance on customizing research parameters, extending topic analysis capabilities, and managing community interactions. Document the relationship between research findings and debate content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blockchain Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive blockchain integration documentation focusing on the Solana token system and meme coin infrastructure. Explain the SPL token integration, balance verification processes, and transaction handling for the $DISS token. Document the tokenomics, supply distribution, and economic model behind the meme coin. Include the launch kit components covering domain recommendations, social media content creation, and marketing automation tools. Provide examples of wallet integration, token balance checking, and stake management workflows. Address security considerations for blockchain interactions, smart contract integration points, and community engagement strategies. Document the relationship between blockchain activities and platform access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation covering all public endpoints and interfaces. Document the debate API endpoints for initiating debates, real-time streaming events, and parameter validation. Include the staking API for tier information, status checking, and wallet verification. Cover the research API for topic analysis, web search integration, and evidence synthesis. Document the metrics API for analytics data and transparency reporting. Provide detailed endpoint specifications including HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Include practical examples, client implementation guidelines, and rate limiting information. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and integration best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation covering both development and production environments. Document the VPS deployment process using PowerShell scripts, nginx configuration, systemd service management, and SSL certificate setup. Include environment variable configuration, reverse proxy setup, and security hardening procedures. Provide guidance on automated deployment pipelines, monitoring setup, log management, and performance optimization. Address scaling considerations, backup strategies, and disaster recovery procedures. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, environment-specific configurations, and maintenance procedures. Document the relationship between different deployment targets (development, staging, production).

wiki_catalogs.name: Customization & Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed customization and extension documentation for modifying and extending the Dissensus platform. Document the agent customization system including personality definitions, system prompts, and visual styling options. Explain the theme and branding customization through CSS modifications, logo integration, and color scheme changes. Include guidance on adding new AI providers, creating custom debate agents, and extending the research engine capabilities. Provide examples of plugin development, API extensions, and frontend component customization. Address configuration management, environment-specific customizations, and maintaining compatibility during updates. Document the extensibility points and best practices for long-term maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and frequently asked questions. Document solutions for API integration problems, deployment failures, performance issues, and user support scenarios. Include debugging techniques for the debate engine, staking system, and blockchain integration. Provide step-by-step resolution guides for environment setup problems, dependency conflicts, and configuration errors. Address performance optimization strategies, memory management, and resource usage monitoring. Include diagnostic procedures for identifying bottlenecks, error patterns, and system health issues. Document rollback procedures, emergency fixes, and escalation paths for complex problems. Provide FAQ sections covering user questions, developer concerns, and operational challenges.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Dissensus — $DISS Meme Coin & AI Debate Platform

## Project Introduction

Dissensus is a revolutionary AI-powered debate platform that brings three distinct artificial intelligence agents into a structured dialectical process. The project combines a meme coin ($DISS) with a sophisticated AI debate engine, creating a unique ecosystem where blockchain governance meets adversarial truth-finding.

**Core Goals:**
- **Truth-Finding**: Forge consensus from chaos through structured AI debate
- **Community Building**: $DISS token holders govern platform evolution
- **Utility Evolution**: Meme coin transforms into platform access and governance token
- **Educational Impact**: Provide transparent, evidence-based analysis on any topic

**Target Audience:**
- Crypto enthusiasts seeking educational content
- AI researchers exploring debate methodologies
- Community members wanting to participate in platform governance
- Developers interested in AI debate architectures

## Technical Architecture

The Dissensus platform follows a microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and blockchain integration layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Landing Page] --> B[Frontend App]
    B --> C[Debate Engine API]
    C --> D[AI Providers]
    C --> E[Staking System]
    C --> F[Metrics System]
    E --> G[Solana Blockchain]
    H[Forum Engine] --> I[Research API]
    I --> J[Web Search]
    K[Token Generation] --> L[Social Media Kit]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **Debate Engine**: Multi-agent AI debate system with 4-phase methodology
- **Staking System**: Token gating and access control mechanism
- **Metrics Dashboard**: Transparency and analytics platform
- **Solana Integration**: On-chain $DISS balance verification
- **Card Generator**: Social media shareable content creation
- **Forum Engine**: Research-powered discussion platform

**Design Patterns:**
- **Microservices Architecture**: Separate concerns across distinct services
- **Provider Pattern**: Pluggable AI model integrations
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time event streaming for debate progress
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic agent instantiation and configuration

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant FE as Frontend
    participant API as Debate API
    participant DE as Debate Engine
    participant AI as AI Provider
    participant SB as Solana Balance
    
    U->>FE: Submit Topic
    FE->>API: Validate Request
    API->>SB: Verify $DISS Balance
    SB-->>API: Balance Info
    API->>DE: Start Debate
    DE->>AI: Request Analysis
    AI-->>DE: Agent Responses
    DE-->>FE: Stream Results
    FE-->>U: Display Verdict
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Server** (`dissensus-engine/server/index.js`)
- Express.js application with production-ready middleware
- SSE streaming for real-time debate progress
- Comprehensive rate limiting and security headers
- Modular API endpoints for all platform features

**Frontend Application** (`dissensus-engine/public/index.html`)
- Single-page application with React-like architecture
- Real-time debate visualization with agent-specific styling
- Responsive design optimized for mobile and desktop
- Integrated wallet connectivity and staking controls

### Core Modules

**Debate Engine** (`dissensus-engine/server/debate-engine.js`)
- Implements 4-phase dialectical process
- Parallel agent execution for efficiency
- Structured prompts for each agent personality
- Real-time streaming of debate progression

**Agent System** (`dissensus-engine/server/agents.js`)
- **CIPHER**: The Skeptic - adversarial red team auditor
- **NOVA**: The Advocate - blue-sky opportunity finder  
- **PRISM**: The Synthesizer - neutral analyst and referee

**Staking System** (`dissensus-engine/server/staking.js`)
- Tier-based access control (FREE, BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, WHALE)
- Daily debate limits with progressive access
- Simulated staking for demonstration purposes
- Integration with Solana blockchain for production

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables** (`dissensus-engine/.env.example`)
- API keys for OpenAI, DeepSeek, and Google Gemini
- Solana RPC endpoints and token mint addresses
- Security settings including trust proxy configuration
- Rate limiting and staking enforcement parameters

**Deployment Configuration** (`dissensus-engine/deploy-env-to-vps.ps1`)
- PowerShell script for automated VPS deployment
- Environment variable management
- Service configuration for systemd

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`dissensus-engine/package.json`)
- **Express.js**: Web application framework
- **@solana/web3.js**: Solana blockchain integration
- **@resvg/resvg-js**: SVG to PNG conversion for cards
- **satori**: HTML to SVG rendering engine
- **helmet**: Security headers protection
- **express-rate-limit**: Rate limiting middleware

**Frontend Dependencies**
- Inter and JetBrains Mono fonts for consistent typography
- Modern CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

### Integration Points

**AI Provider Integrations**
- OpenAI GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini
- DeepSeek V3.2 with competitive pricing
- Google Gemini 2.0 Flash with free tier access

**Blockchain Integration**
- Solana mainnet token balance verification
- Associated token account (ATA) resolution
- Program ID configuration for future staking program

**External Services**
- CoinGecko for trending cryptocurrency topics
- DuckDuckGo for web research capabilities
- GitHub Pages for documentation hosting

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Debate Engine] --> B[CIPHER Agent]
    A --> C[NOVA Agent]
    A --> D[PRISM Agent]
    A --> E[AI Providers]
    F[Staking System] --> G[Wallet Validation]
    H[Metrics System] --> I[Usage Analytics]
    J[Card Generator] --> K[Social Sharing]
    L[Forum Engine] --> M[Research API]
    M --> N[Web Search]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**AI Debate System**
- **4-Phase Methodology**: Independent analysis → Opening arguments → Cross-examination → Final verdict
- **Adversarial Truth-Finding**: Three agents with opposing perspectives challenge each other's claims
- **Structured Output**: Clear, ranked conclusions with confidence levels and remaining disagreements
- **Real-Time Streaming**: Server-Sent Events provide instant feedback during debate execution

**Token Integration**
- **$DISS Meme Coin**: Fair launch with zero pre-mine on Solana
- **Utility Evolution**: From meme coin → access token → governance token
- **Staking-Based Access**: Token holdings determine debate limits and premium features
- **Governance Participation**: $DISS holders vote on platform development and agent personalities

**Social Media Integration**
- **Shareable Cards**: Twitter-optimized debate summaries with recommended lists
- **Automated Content Creation**: Dynamic card generation with blockchain verification
- **Community Engagement**: Meme campaigns and social media presence

### Implementation Highlights

**Debate Visualization** (`dissensus-engine/public/js/app.js`)
- Real-time agent status indicators with color-coded progress
- Interactive debate arena with agent-specific styling
- Copy-to-clipboard functionality for easy sharing
- Wallet connectivity with Solana integration

**Research Capabilities** (`forum/server.py`)
- Multi-angle topic analysis with domain-specific search strategies
- Automated fact extraction and synthesis
- Domain classification for specialized research approaches
- Evidence-based argument construction

**Metrics Dashboard** (`dissensus-engine/public/metrics.html`)
- Real-time platform usage statistics
- Provider utilization tracking
- Community engagement metrics
- Transparent governance participation data

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Landing
    Landing --> Authenticated: Connect Wallet
    Authenticated --> Debate: Submit Topic
    Debate --> Streaming: Start Debate
    Streaming --> Verdict: Complete Debate
    Verdict --> Card: Generate Shareable Card
    Card --> [*]: Share Content
    Verdict --> [*]: Copy/Save Results
    Authenticated --> Forum: Research Discussion
    Forum --> [*]: Engage Community
```

**Development Roadmap Integration**
- **Phase 1**: Launch & Community - meme coin and landing page
- **Phase 2**: Platform Live - debate engine with $DISS branding
- **Phase 3**: Premium & Burn - utility features with token burning
- **Phase 4**: Governance - community-driven platform evolution

**Performance Considerations**
- **Rate Limiting**: Prevents abuse while maintaining responsive user experience
- **Caching Strategy**: Trending topics cached per day for CoinGecko integration
- **Memory Management**: In-memory metrics with periodic cleanup
- **Scalability**: Modular architecture allows horizontal scaling of AI providers

The Dissensus platform represents a unique intersection of blockchain technology, artificial intelligence, and community governance, creating a novel approach to truth-finding and educational content delivery.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [package.json](dissensus-engine/package.json)
- [index.js](dissensus-engine/server/index.js)
- [debate-engine.js](dissensus-engine/server/debate-engine.js)
- [agents.js](dissensus-engine/server/agents.js)
- [index.html](dissensus-engine/public/index.html)
- [server.py](forum/server.py)
- [staking.js](dissensus-engine/server/staking.js)
- [metrics.js](dissensus-engine/server/metrics.js)
- [solana-balance.js](dissensus-engine/server/solana-balance.js)
- [card-generator.js](dissensus-engine/server/card-generator.js)
- [debate-of-the-day.js](dissensus-engine/server/debate-of-the-day.js)

</blog>
